description: Missing YAML frontmatter (name: and description:) AND missing output format specification — {output_schema} placeholder has no default schema defined in-file; callers cannot rely on a stable output shape
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter with name: and description:; define a default JSON schema for {output_schema} or add a variable contract section documenting required keys

description: Missing > **Paths:** header present on all other skills-catalog SKILL.md files; violates skill_contract.md path-resolution rule — agents may fail to locate referenced files when CWD != repo root
suggested_fix: Add > **Paths:** header block immediately after frontmatter, listing all referenced paths relative to skills repo root

description: eval "$cmd" in run_check() executes the second argument as a shell command; any caller supplying a malicious second argument achieves arbitrary code execution — template is designed for developer customization so the attack surface is real
suggested_fix: Replace eval with an array-based execution pattern; validate command names against an allowlist before execution; disclose privately before any public PR

description: --dangerously-skip-permissions passed to claude CLI for built-in session; bypasses Claude Code permission model — unsafe if the script is run against untrusted project trees
suggested_fix: Guard behind BENCH_ALLOW_DANGEROUS=1 env var; document the constraint in the script header comment

description: Second --dangerously-skip-permissions in hex-line session invocation — same risk as line 164; both occurrences must be guarded together
suggested_fix: Same guard as line 164: only allow when BENCH_ALLOW_DANGEROUS=1 is explicitly set by the caller

description: git commit --no-gpg-sign --no-verify in checkpoint_worktree() skips signing and pre-commit hooks on benchmark worktree commits; low risk in isolated worktrees but sets a permissive pattern
suggested_fix: Add inline comment documenting the isolation context; acceptable inside benchmark worktrees but should not propagate to other git wrappers

description: All dependencies use ^ semver ranges (e.g. @modelcontextprotocol/sdk: ^1.29.0); minor/patch upgrades are auto-pulled in distributed MCP servers without explicit lockfile review
suggested_fix: Pin exact versions or commit package-lock.json to the mcp/ workspace and enforce lockfile review in CI before publishing

description: No example blocks (input→output usage demos) present in any of the 100 audited SKILL.md or prompt template files
suggested_fix: Add ## Examples section with at least one concrete input→output pair to each SKILL.md

description: No model: declaration; NLPM cannot select appropriate tier for standalone invocations
suggested_fix: Add model: sonnet or model: haiku to frontmatter based on task complexity

description: No model: declaration
suggested_fix: Add model: declaration to frontmatter

suggested_fix: Add model: declaration to frontmatter

description: No model: declaration
suggested_fix: Add model: declaration to frontmatter

description: No model: declaration in any of the 68 Codex adapter SKILL.md files in plugins/; thin adapters rely on canonical skill model but NLPM cannot select tier for direct adapter invocations
suggested_fix: Add model: declaration matching the canonical skill's model, or document that adapter invocations inherit model from the canonical skill

description: {output_schema} is an unresolved placeholder — callers must supply the schema at render time but no default or in-file contract is documented; callers cannot rely on a stable output shape
suggested_fix: Define a default JSON schema inline or add a variable contract section listing required output keys and their types

description: Uses 18+ template variables ({mode_header}, {mode_constraints}, {mode_body}, etc.) with no variable contract document; the composition contract between review_base.md and the mode files is entirely implicit
suggested_fix: Add a variables_contract.md or inline variable table listing all template slots, their types, and valid values; reference it from review_base.md

description: Mode files use ## header, ## constraints, ## body markers that act as assembly keys for review_base.md but the protocol is undocumented; these are not standard Markdown headings and will confuse unaware editors
suggested_fix: Document the assembly key convention in a modes/README.md or in the review_base.md variable contract section

description: No empty/null $ARGUMENTS handling defined; command always runs against current repo — acceptable for a repo-scoped command but undocumented
suggested_fix: Add a note in the command description confirming that empty-args behavior (run against current repo) is intentional; no code change needed

description: Glob declared in allowed-tools but not directly invoked — Bash covers all file inspection; inflates the tool surface unnecessarily
suggested_fix: Remove Glob from allowed-tools if all file inspection is performed via Bash

description: Uses 'appropriate' (Phase 2 bottleneck section) and 'relevant' (Phase 3 local check) — vague quantifiers that leave agent behavior undefined
suggested_fix: Replace with measurable criteria: e.g. 'top 3 bottlenecks by query count' instead of 'appropriate bottlenecks'

description: 'relevant' appears twice in Detection sections — vague quantifier that leaves detection scope undefined
suggested_fix: Replace with specific criteria defining what makes a query relevant to each detection check

description: 'appropriate' in Phase 3 merge strategy description
suggested_fix: Specify the merge strategy algorithm or decision criteria explicitly rather than deferring to 'appropriate'

description: 'appropriate' appears in Phase 4 timing section
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete timing values or a deterministic formula

description: 'relevant' in Layer 2 context analysis steps
suggested_fix: Define explicit criteria for which context items qualify as relevant to the analysis

description: All 12 prompt templates under skills-catalog/shared/agents/prompt_templates/ have no YAML frontmatter and cannot be indexed by NL artifact scanners; variable contract between review_base.md and mode files is implicit — missing variables produce silent {placeholder} literals in rendered prompts
suggested_fix: Add minimal frontmatter (name:, description:) to each template; add a variables_contract.md defining all {slot} names and valid values

description: All 68 Codex adapter SKILL.md files carry Version: 1.0.0 regardless of canonical skill version; when canonical skills update the adapters show no drift signal and version numbers become meaningless
suggested_fix: Track canonical skill version in adapter Last Updated: date, or add a canonical_version: frontmatter field mirroring the canonical SKILL.md version

description: Skills ln-160, ln-140, ln-115, and ln-112 exist in both skills-catalog/ (canonical) and plugins/documentation-pipeline/ (adapter); description text must stay in sync — currently consistent but at risk on next canonical update
suggested_fix: Add a CI lint step that diffs canonical vs. adapter description fields on every commit that touches skills-catalog/

description: Worker Invocation section (line 310) references optimization-runtime/cli.mjs but the Runtime Contract section at the top of the same file references dependency-runtime/cli.mjs — one path is stale
suggested_fix: Verify which CLI path is correct for worker delegation and remove or update the stale reference; add the correct path to the > **Paths:** block
